questions.q: The Gupta Empire flourished mainly during which centuries CE?
questions.explanation: The Gupta Empire rose in the early 4th century CE and declined by the 6th century CE.

questions.q: The Gupta period is often described as a classical golden age of what?
questions.explanation: The Gupta era is celebrated as a golden age of Indian civilization for its achievements in arts and sciences.

questions.q: In which broad part of the subcontinent was the Gupta Empire centred?
questions.explanation: The Guptas were centred in northern India, with their heartland in the Ganges valley.

questions.q: Which fields of learning especially flourished under the Guptas?
questions.explanation: The Gupta age saw major advances in mathematics, astronomy, literature and art.

questions.q: Who is regarded as the ruler that raised the Gupta family to imperial status?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta I elevated the Guptas to imperial rank in the early 4th century CE.

questions.q: Which emperor, nicknamed the 'Indian Napoleon', greatly expanded the Gupta realm?
questions.explanation: Samudragupta's wide military campaigns earned him the modern nickname the 'Indian Napoleon'.

questions.q: The Gupta Empire is symbolised in this deck by which floral emblem?
questions.explanation: The lotus emblem represents the cultural flowering of the classical Gupta golden age.

questions.q: Which city served as a principal capital of the Gupta state in northern India?
questions.explanation: Pataliputra, the old Magadhan and Mauryan capital, was also a key Gupta capital.

questions.q: Which emperor took the title Vikramaditya and defeated the Western Kshatrapas?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta II adopted the title Vikramaditya and crushed the Western Kshatrapas of Malwa and Gujarat.

questions.q: The Gupta Empire is best known for advances in art alongside which kind of literature?
questions.explanation: Classical Sanskrit literature flourished under the Guptas, including the works attributed to Kalidasa.

questions.q: The great mathematician-astronomer Aryabhata, who wrote the Aryabhatiya, lived during which empire?
questions.explanation: Aryabhata composed the Aryabhatiya in 499 CE during the Gupta age, advancing astronomy and mathematics.

questions.q: Which celebrated Sanskrit poet and dramatist is traditionally associated with the Gupta court?
questions.explanation: Kalidasa, author of Shakuntala and Meghaduta, is traditionally linked to the Gupta golden age.

questions.q: The Chinese pilgrim who visited India during the Gupta period and described it was?
questions.explanation: Faxian travelled across Gupta India around 400 CE and left an account of its prosperity and administration.

questions.q: Which invading people contributed heavily to the decline of the Gupta Empire?
questions.explanation: Repeated invasions by the Hunas (White Huns) in the 5th and 6th centuries CE weakened and fragmented the Gupta Empire.

questions.q: The Guptas are famed for issuing fine coins primarily made of which metal?
questions.explanation: Gupta gold coins, called dinaras, are admired for their artistry and depictions of rulers and deities.

questions.q: The famous Iron Pillar now in Delhi, noted for resisting rust, dates to which era?
questions.explanation: The rustless Iron Pillar bears a Sanskrit inscription honouring a king Chandra and is dated to the Gupta period.

questions.q: Which monastic university flourished as a major centre of learning during and after the Gupta age?
questions.explanation: Nalanda Mahavihara grew into a renowned Buddhist university, receiving Gupta patronage.

questions.q: The decimal place-value system and the concept of zero matured in India during which classical age?
questions.explanation: Indian mathematics, including the place-value system and use of zero, advanced significantly in the Gupta period.

questions.q: The famous Buddhist murals patronised in part during the Gupta era are found at which caves?
questions.explanation: Many of the celebrated Ajanta cave paintings belong to the Gupta-era Vakataka phase of the 5th century CE.

questions.q: Which dynasty in the Deccan was closely allied with the Guptas through a marriage of Chandragupta II's daughter Prabhavatigupta?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta II married his daughter Prabhavatigupta to the Vakataka king, forging a key southern alliance.

questions.q: Which late Gupta ruler is credited with repelling the Huna invader Mihirakula?
questions.explanation: Skandagupta checked early Huna incursions, and the ruler Yashodharman later defeated Mihirakula.

questions.q: Gupta land administration commonly recorded grants of land to brahmanas and temples on what medium?
questions.explanation: Gupta and post-Gupta land grants were frequently inscribed on copper plates, a vital source for the period.

questions.q: Samudragupta's far-reaching conquests are recorded in an inscription composed by his court poet on a pillar at which site?
questions.explanation: The Allahabad (Prayag Prashasti) pillar inscription by Harishena details Samudragupta's campaigns and was engraved on a pillar originally raised by Ashoka.

questions.q: Which Gupta ruler is generally regarded as the founder of the dynasty, preceding Chandragupta I and known from the title Maharaja?
questions.explanation: Sri Gupta is named as the dynasty's progenitor; his line achieved imperial status only with Chandragupta I.

questions.q: Kumaragupta I, a 5th-century Gupta emperor, is traditionally credited with founding which great learning centre?
questions.explanation: Tradition and epigraphy associate the foundation of Nalanda with the reign of Kumaragupta I.

questions.q: The Bhitari pillar inscription primarily records the achievements of which Gupta ruler, including his fight against the Hunas?
questions.explanation: The Bhitari pillar inscription celebrates Skandagupta and his resistance to the Hunas and the Pushyamitras.

questions.q: Gupta gold coins frequently depict rulers performing royal activities; one common type shows the king doing what?
options: Hunting a lion or playing the veena
questions.explanation: Gupta coin types include the king as lion-slayer and Samudragupta playing the veena, reflecting both valour and culture.

questions.q: The Sanskrit astronomical-mathematical text 'Surya Siddhanta' took important shape during which broad period?
questions.explanation: The Surya Siddhanta, a foundational text of Indian astronomy, was consolidated during the Gupta-classical era.

questions.q: Which obscure Gupta ruler, named in the play Devichandraguptam, is said to have been overthrown by Chandragupta II?
questions.explanation: Literary tradition holds that Chandragupta II succeeded after the weak Ramagupta, who reportedly capitulated to the Shakas.

questions.q: By what name did the Guptas style their realm's high sovereigns, a grand imperial title also used by Chandragupta I?
questions.explanation: Gupta emperors used the title Maharajadhiraja, 'great king of kings', signalling their imperial standing.
